bullets.text: Q3 CR just under 93, comparable to prior year Q3 and YTD; Commercial Lines CR 85 with 20% NPW growth — extended remarkable combination into a second consecutive quarter; Property CR ~92 including ~7 pts amortization.

Direct Personal Lines growth in low teens; Agency auto returning to growth in 6%+ range after sustained underperformance; Commercial Lines NPW growing 20%; Property premiums written nearly $224M in Q3 as Platinum rollout expands Robinson growth.

Agency new business applications increased markedly in Q3 with pleasing geographic dispersion; Direct auto PIF growth ~8%; Platinum home+auto bundled offering expanded to 8 states (Texas plus 7 additional during Q3) with early results described as 'very satisfactory.'

Loss frequency and severity trends rising; current results reflect pricing from earlier periods; modest rate adjustments building as necessary; Direct retention strong while Agency retention stubbornly slow to improve despite new business gains; 3-month trailing retention measure showing early signs of acceleration.

Loss frequency and severity trends rising (details in 10-Q); special lines enjoyed another favorable weather quarter with loss costs slightly below expectation; no major storm affecting boats or RVs; Snapshot UBI data used to monitor mileage and driving behavior trends in real-time.

Q3 equity markets down and highly volatile; investment strategy not requiring position changes; underwriting capacity remains the protected capital asset; strong underwriting performance generating capital to support growth plans.

Commercial Lines expected to reach #1 commercial auto market share when industrywide results tabulated; Agency personal lines market showing rate actions across all competitors creating shopping environment favorable for Progressive.

Snapshot program data continuously informing pricing adjustments; Platinum (integrated ASI+Progressive home/auto) rolled out to 8 states during Q3; multi-product customers in the low teens as percentage of book — 'destination company' transformation ongoing.

Agency channel showing clearest improvement — new business applications up markedly in Q3 after multi-point response agenda from May Investor Conference; not yet policy accretive to prior-year levels but direction changed; Direct channel momentum sustained alongside Agency recovery.

Snapshot UBI used for real-time trend analysis beyond pricing — mileage types and associated frequency monitored to distinguish weather effects from underlying trends; mobile capabilities expanding; Big Data initiative with special CEO oversight funding advancing data science capabilities.
